title: NYT analysis: White House weighs SOF raid on Iran's enriched uranium stockpile
summary: New York Times World analysis assesses the White House review of military options against Iran scheduled for April 30, including a potential special operations forces raid to seize and remove Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile, against the backdrop of an ongoing US naval blockade and stranded Iranian crude exports.
contents:
# NYT analysis: White House weighs SOF raid on Iran's enriched uranium stockpile

## Reporting Substance
New York Times World correspondents drew on US officials and Washington Post reporting to outline the menu of military options CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper plans to brief President Trump on April 30. Among the options under review: a special operations forces raid intended to seize and remove Iran's highly enriched uranium (HEU) stockpile from one or more known nuclear facilities, an action that would mark the most direct US move against Iran's nuclear infrastructure since the campaign began.

## Strategic Stakes
The NYT analysis emphasises that even a tightly scoped raid would carry severe escalation risk, including potential Iranian retaliation against US bases in Iraq, Syria, the Gulf, and against US-flagged shipping. The analysis frames the SOF option against ongoing economic pressure: 42 vessels redirected from Iranian ports, 41 tankers and 69 million barrels worth $6 billion stranded offshore, and Iran's President Pezeshkian publicly rejecting the blockade as illegal.

## Domestic and Allied Reaction
The analysis flags emerging Congressional skepticism on both sides regarding open-ended military escalation, alongside European allies' concerns about humanitarian and oil-market consequences. Brent crude reached $126 per barrel earlier April 30 on speculation about additional US strikes.

## Tags
nytimes, iran, sof-raid, heu, centcom, brad-cooper, blockade, escalation
